God shrines and regional dedications

HomeGetting StartedAdventurer LevelsPlanar RiftsBreach GatesWorld BossesCreaturesLoot and RelicsShrines and LoreBounty BoardsReference

God Shrines and Lore

Nosivad replaces standard resurrection shrines with god shrines tied to regional identity, resurrection, and one-hour blessings.

How Shrines Work

  • Dead players can use shrines to resurrect.
  • Living players can commune for a deity blessing.
  • Each city, town, and dungeon entrance should have access to a shrine.
  • Regions use a single dedication so the divine identity of the area is clear.

Pantheon

BahamutCorellonHelmLolthMoradinPelorTorm

Shrine Blessings

Communing at a shrine grants a one-hour blessing. Only one shrine blessing can be active at a time; communing at another shrine replaces the previous blessing.

DeityBlessingBest For
Bahamut+10 defense and +10% maximum health.Durable frontline adventurers and dangerous travel.
Corellon+8 accuracy and +2 mana regeneration.Archers, casters, and precision-focused builds.
Helm+5 defense chance and +8% maximum health.Cautious dungeon runs and defensive play.
Lolth+8% move speed and +8% attack power.Fast skirmishers and aggressive solo play.
Moradin+14 defense and +2 vitality regeneration.Heavy armor, shield users, and long fights.
Pelor+4 health regeneration and +15% healing received.Recovery, group support, and undead-heavy areas.
Torm+10% attack power and +8 defense.Starter-town adventures, balanced combat, and first rifts.

Current Dedications

RegionDedicationFeel
Southern RimPelorLight, healing, refuge.
Barren LandsBahamutEndurance and protection in harsh lands.
Black ForestCorellonWild beauty and old paths.
Eastern FrontierHelmWatchfulness and guarded roads.
Southern HillsMoradinStone, craft, and settlement.
Upper PlainsTormDuty, courage, and starter-town guidance.
CatacombsPelorLight against the dead below.
MonolithCorellonAncient mystery and planar echoes.

Shard Premise

Rifts are wounds in the boundary between worlds. Shrines represent the mortal attempt to anchor meaning, safety, and divine order against the pressure of those breaches.

Nosivad Wiki
